title: Quality + adoption metrics: rubric scores, reviewer-corrected rate, error mix, installs/ratings
context: To evaluate whether the open-flashcards pipeline is producing high-quality, widely-used content, structured quality and adoption metrics must be collected and tracked. Rubric scores and reviewer-corrected rates measure pipeline quality; install counts and ratings (where available) measure learner adoption. This dataset is the sustainability metric for the project.
objective: Collect, structure, and publish quality and adoption metrics for the published deck set, including per-deck rubric scores, reviewer-corrected rates, error-taxonomy mix, and available install/rating data.
acceptanceCriteria: Collects per-deck accuracy rubric scores (4-dimension averages) and reviewer-corrected rate (cards changed / cards reviewed) for all published decks.
Collects error-taxonomy mix (counts per error category per deck) from reviewer records.
Collects available adoption data (install counts, ratings, or download stats) from the publishing channel(s).
Structures metrics as a versioned JSON or CSV dataset in metrics/ with a documented schema.
Includes a summary table (metrics/summary.md) showing trends across the batch-001 deck set.
Dataset licensed CC0-1.0 (metrics are facts, not creative works).
output: A versioned metrics dataset (metrics/quality-adoption.json or .csv) with schema documentation and a summary table (metrics/summary.md), licensed CC0-1.0.
